prompt: Ukiyo-e Japanese woodblock print style, Edo period, Hiroshige composition. A narrow mountain path winds through a landscape. A lone traveler crouches obsessively on one small section of the path, painstakingly tracing every tiny crack, pebble, and imperfection in the stones with their finger. Behind them, the path stretches broadly into a vast open landscape — mountains, rivers, sky — completely unexplored and ignored. Flat unmodulated areas of color — indigo, vermillion, ochre, cream, black. Bold black outlines. Visible woodgrain texture throughout. No photographic shading. No gradients. Asymmetric composition with strong diagonal. Flat spatial organization. Traditional washi paper texture. No text, no labels, single frame.
tradition_approach: Flat unmodulated color fields in indigo, vermillion and ochre, bold black outlines, visible woodgrain texture, asymmetric diagonal composition, zero photographic shading — all core Edo-period woodblock print grammar.
